revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''天満天神宮'''（てんまんてんじんぐう）は、[[千葉県]][[市川市]]にある[[日蓮宗]]の[[寺院]]の本光寺 (市川市)|本光...'
revision.text:
'''天満天神宮'''（てんまんてんじんぐう）は、[[千葉県]][[市川市]]にある[[日蓮宗]]の[[寺院]]の[[本光寺 (市川市)|本光寺]]が別当。
== [[本光寺 (市川市)|本光寺]]古文書 == [[1778年]]（[[安永(元号)|安永]]7年）、[[本光寺 (市川市)|本光寺]]の第19世寛具院[[日正]]([[日荘]])により[[天神]]を再興。 [[1785年]]（[[天明(元号)|天明]]5年）2月より3月まで、天満天神宮の屋根替。

'''天満天神宮'''（てんまんてんじんぐう）は、[[千葉県]][[市川市]]にある[[日蓮宗]]の[[寺院]]の[[本光寺 (市川市)|本光寺]]が別当。
== 起源と歴史 == [[938年]]（[[天慶(元号)|天慶]]1年）、[[平将門]]が京都の[[北野天満宮]]をこの地に勧請したものであると伝えられる。菅原道真公の像を描いた掛け軸に、その由来を記したものが残されている。また、[[日蓮聖人]]御開眼とも記されている。
== [[本光寺 (市川市)|本光寺]]古文書 == [[1778年]]（[[安永(元号)|安永]]7年）、[[本光寺 (市川市)|本光寺]]の第19世寛具院[[日正]]([[日荘]])により[[天神]]を再興。 [[1785年]]（[[天明(元号)|天明]]5年）2月より3月まで、天満天神宮の屋根替。

{{複数の問題 |出典の明記=2015年11月7日 (土) 16:06 (UTC) |Wikify=2015年11月7日 (土) 16:06 (UTC) }} '''天満天神宮'''（てんまんてんじんぐう）は、[[千葉県]][[市川市]]にある[[日蓮宗]]の[[寺院]]の[[本光寺 (市川市)|本光寺]]が[[別当]]。
== 起源と歴史 == [[938年]]（[[天慶]]1年）、[[平将門]]が京都の[[北野天満宮]]をこの地に勧請したものであると伝えられる。[[菅原道真]]公の像を描いた[[掛軸]]に、その由来を記したものが残されている。また、[[日蓮]]聖人御開眼とも記されている。
== 本光寺古文書 == *[[1778年]]（[[安永 (元号)|安永]]7年）、[[本光寺 (市川市)|本光寺]]の第19世寛具院[[日正]]([[日荘]])により、[[天神]]を再興。 *[[1785年]]（[[天明]]5年）2月より3月まで、[[本光寺 (市川市)|本光寺]]の第19世寛具院[[日正]]([[日荘]])により、天満天神宮の屋根替。
== 関連項目 == *[[大野城 (下総国)]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.honkouji.com/houmu/kigan/goukaku/index 本光寺ホームページ（合格祈願）]
{{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=千葉県}}
{{デフォルトソート:てんまんてんしんくう}} [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:市川市の歴史]]

== 起源と歴史 == [[938年]]（[[天慶]]1年）、[[平将門]]が京都の[[北野天満宮]]をこの地に勧請したものであると伝えられる。[[菅原道真]]公の像を描いた[[掛軸]]に、その由来を記したものが残されている。また、[[日蓮]]聖人御開眼とも記されている。
== 本光寺古文書 == *[[1778年]]（[[安永 (元号)|安永]]7年）、[[本光寺 (市川市)|本光寺]]の第19世寛具院[[日正]]{{要曖昧さ回避|date=2016年1月}}([[日荘]])により、[[天神]]を再興。 *[[1785年]]（[[天明]]5年）2月より3月まで、本光寺の第19世寛具院日正(日荘)により、天満天神宮の屋根替。
== 関連項目 == *[[大野城 (下総国)]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.honkouji.com/houmu/kigan/goukaku/index 本光寺ホームページ（合格祈願）]

{{Multiple issues | Citation needed = November 7, 2015 | Wikify = November 7, 2015 }} {{Shrine | Name = Tenman Tenjin-gū | Image = [[File:Tenman Tenjin-gū (Ichikawa) 06.jpg|240px]] | Location = {{ill|Ichikawa City|ja|市川市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Ōno-machi 3-1948 | Map = | Deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Rank = | Founded = {{ill|Tengyō|ja|天慶}} 1 ({{ill|938|ja}}) | Main hall = | Alias = | Fuda-sho = | Annual festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (天満天神宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located on the site of the former {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)}} in {{ill|Ichikawa City|ja|市川市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. The shrine is managed by {{ill|Honkōji Temple (Ichikawa)|ja|本光寺 (市川市)}}, a {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|日蓮宗}} temple. It is also known as '''Ōno Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (大野天満天神宮).
== Origins and History ==
According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Tengyō|ja|天慶}} 1 ({{ill|938|ja}}) by {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, who enshrined the deity of the {{ill|Kitano Tenmangū|ja|北野天満宮}} in Kyoto at this location. A hanging scroll depicting {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, which includes a record of this origin, remains preserved. It is also noted that {{ill|Nichiren|ja|日蓮}} performed a dedication ceremony for the shrine. Later, Honkōji Temple became the shrine’s {{ill|bettō|ja|別当}} (caretaker temple), and it has remained under the temple’s management since the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}.
== Honkōji Temple Historical Records ==
* In {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 7 ({{ill|1778|ja}}), the 19th head priest of Honkōji, Kangu-in {{ill|Nichishō (Honkōji)|ja|日正}} (Nichishō), restored the deity {{ill|Tenman Daijizai Tenjin|ja|天満大自在天神}}. * From February to March of {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 5 ({{ill|1785|ja}}), Nichishō (Nichishō) replaced the roof of the Tenman Tenjin-gū.
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)}} * {{ill|Ōno Tenmangū|ja|大野天満宮}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.honkouji.com/houmu/kigan/goukaku/index Honkōji Temple Homepage (Success Prayer)]

{{Multiple issues | Citation needed = November 7, 2015 | Wikify = November 7, 2015 }} {{Shrine | Name = Tenman Tenjin-gū | Image = [[File:Tenman Tenjin-gū (Ichikawa) 06.jpg|240px]] | Location = {{ill|Ichikawa City|ja|市川市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Ōno-machi 3-1948 | Map = | Deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Rank = | Founded = {{ill|Tengyō|ja|天慶}} 1 ({{ill|938|ja}}) | Main hall = | Alias = | Fuda-sho = | Annual festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (天満天神宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located on the site of the former {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)}} in {{ill|Ichikawa City|ja|市川市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. The shrine is managed by {{ill|Honkōji Temple (Ichikawa)|ja|本光寺 (市川市)}}, a {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|日蓮宗}} temple. It is also known as '''Ōno Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (大野天満天神宮).
== Origins and History ==
According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Tengyō|ja|天慶}} 1 ({{ill|938|ja}}) by {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, who enshrined the deity of the {{ill|Kitano Tenmangū|ja|北野天満宮}} in Kyoto at this location. A hanging scroll depicting {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, which includes a record of this origin, remains preserved. It is also noted that {{ill|Nichiren|ja|日蓮}} performed a dedication ceremony for the shrine. Later, Honkōji Temple became the shrine’s {{ill|bettō|ja|別当}} (caretaker temple), and it has remained under the temple’s management since the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}.
== Honkōji Temple Historical Records ==
* In {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 7 ({{ill|1778|ja}}), the 19th head priest of Honkōji, Kangu-in {{ill|Nichishō (Honkōji)|ja|日正}} (Nichishō), restored the deity {{ill|Tenman Daijizai Tenjin|ja|天満大自在天神}}. * From February to March of {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 5 ({{ill|1785|ja}}), Nichishō (Nichishō) replaced the roof of the Tenman Tenjin-gū.
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)}} * {{ill|Ōno Tenmangū|ja|大野天満宮}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.honkouji.com/houmu/kigan/goukaku/index Honkōji Temple Homepage (Success Prayer)]

{{Multiple issues | Citation needed = November 7, 2015 | Wikify = November 7, 2015 }} {{Shrine | name = Tenman Tenjin-gū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tenman Tenjin-gū (Ichikawa) 06.jpg|240px]] | location = {{ill|Ichikawa City|ja|市川市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Ōno-machi 3-1948 | Map = | Deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Rank = | consecration_year = {{ill|Tengyō|ja|天慶}} 1 ({{ill|938|ja}}) | Main hall = | Alias = | Fuda-sho = | Annual festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (天満天神宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located on the site of the former {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)}} in {{ill|Ichikawa City|ja|市川市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. The shrine is managed by {{ill|Honkōji Temple (Ichikawa)|ja|本光寺 (市川市)}}, a {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|日蓮宗}} temple. It is also known as '''Ōno Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (大野天満天神宮).
== Origins and History ==
According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Tengyō|ja|天慶}} 1 ({{ill|938|ja}}) by {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, who enshrined the deity of the {{ill|Kitano Tenmangū|ja|北野天満宮}} in Kyoto at this location. A hanging scroll depicting {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, which includes a record of this origin, remains preserved. It is also noted that {{ill|Nichiren|ja|日蓮}} performed a dedication ceremony for the shrine. Later, Honkōji Temple became the shrine’s {{ill|bettō|ja|別当}} (caretaker temple), and it has remained under the temple’s management since the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}.
== Honkōji Temple Historical Records ==
* In {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 7 ({{ill|1778|ja}}), the 19th head priest of Honkōji, Kangu-in {{ill|Nichishō (Honkōji)|ja|日正}} (Nichishō), restored the deity {{ill|Tenman Daijizai Tenjin|ja|天満大自在天神}}. * From February to March of {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 5 ({{ill|1785|ja}}), Nichishō (Nichishō) replaced the roof of the Tenman Tenjin-gū.
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)}} * {{ill|Ōno Tenmangū|ja|大野天満宮}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.honkouji.com/houmu/kigan/goukaku/index Honkōji Temple Homepage (Success Prayer)]

'''Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (天満天神宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located on the site of the former {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)}} in {{ill|Ichikawa City|ja|市川市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. The shrine is managed by {{ill|Honkōji Temple (Ichikawa)|ja|本光寺 (市川市)}}, a {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|日蓮宗}} temple. It is also known as '''Ōno Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (大野天満天神宮).
== Origins and History ==
According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Tengyō|ja|天慶}} 1 ({{ill|938|ja}}) by {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, who enshrined the deity of the {{ill|Kitano Tenmangū|ja|北野天満宮}} in Kyoto at this location. A hanging scroll depicting {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, which includes a record of this origin, remains preserved. It is also noted that {{ill|Nichiren|ja|日蓮}} performed a dedication ceremony for the shrine. Later, Honkōji Temple became the shrine’s {{ill|bettō|ja|別当}} (caretaker temple), and it has remained under the temple’s management since the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}.
== Honkōji Temple Historical Records ==
* In {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 7 ({{ill|1778|ja}}), the 19th head priest of Honkōji, Kangu-in {{ill|Nichishō (Honkōji)|ja|日正}} (Nichishō), restored the deity {{ill|Tenman Daijizai Tenjin|ja|天満大自在天神}}. * From February to March of {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 5 ({{ill|1785|ja}}), Nichishō (Nichishō) replaced the roof of the Tenman Tenjin-gū.
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)}} * {{ill|Ōno Tenmangū|ja|大野天満宮}}

{{Multiple issues | Citation needed = November 7, 2015 | Wikify = November 7, 2015 }} {{Shrine | name = Tenman Tenjin-gū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tenman Tenjin-gū (Ichikawa) 06.jpg|240px]] | location = {{ill|Ichikawa City|ja|市川市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Ōno-machi 3-1948 | Map = | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Rank = | consecration_year = {{ill|Tengyō|ja|天慶}} 1 ({{ill|938|ja}}) | Main hall = | Alias = | Fuda-sho = | Annual festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (天満天神宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located on the site of the former {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)}} in {{ill|Ichikawa City|ja|市川市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. The shrine is managed by {{ill|Honkōji Temple (Ichikawa)|ja|本光寺 (市川市)}}, a {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|日蓮宗}} temple. It is also known as '''Ōno Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (大野天満天神宮).
== Origins and History ==
According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Tengyō|ja|天慶}} 1 ({{ill|938|ja}}) by {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, who enshrined the deity of the {{ill|Kitano Tenmangū|ja|北野天満宮}} in Kyoto at this location. A hanging scroll depicting {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, which includes a record of this origin, remains preserved. It is also noted that {{ill|Nichiren|ja|日蓮}} performed a dedication ceremony for the shrine. Later, Honkōji Temple became the shrine’s {{ill|bettō|ja|別当}} (caretaker temple), and it has remained under the temple’s management since the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}.
== Honkōji Temple Historical Records ==
* In {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 7 ({{ill|1778|ja}}), the 19th head priest of Honkōji, Kangu-in {{ill|Nichishō (Honkōji)|ja|日正}} (Nichishō), restored the deity {{ill|Tenman Daijizai Tenjin|ja|天満大自在天神}}. * From February to March of {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 5 ({{ill|1785|ja}}), Nichishō (Nichishō) replaced the roof of the Tenman Tenjin-gū.
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)}} * {{ill|Ōno Tenmangū|ja|大野天満宮}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.honkouji.com/houmu/kigan/goukaku/index Honkōji Temple Homepage (Success Prayer)]

'''Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (天満天神宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located on the site of the former {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)}} in {{ill|Ichikawa City|ja|市川市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. The shrine is managed by {{ill|Honkōji Temple (Ichikawa)|ja|本光寺 (市川市)}}, a {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|日蓮宗}} temple. It is also known as '''Ōno Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (大野天満天神宮).
== Origins and History ==
According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Tengyō|ja|天慶}} 1 ({{ill|938|ja}}) by {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, who enshrined the deity of the {{ill|Kitano Tenmangū|ja|北野天満宮}} in Kyoto at this location. A hanging scroll depicting {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, which includes a record of this origin, remains preserved. It is also noted that {{ill|Nichiren|ja|日蓮}} performed a dedication ceremony for the shrine. Later, Honkōji Temple became the shrine’s {{ill|bettō|ja|別当}} (caretaker temple), and it has remained under the temple’s management since the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}.
== Honkōji Temple Historical Records ==
* In {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 7 ({{ill|1778|ja}}), the 19th head priest of Honkōji, Kangu-in {{ill|Nichishō (Honkōji)|ja|日正}} (Nichishō), restored the deity {{ill|Tenman Daijizai Tenjin|ja|天満大自在天神}}. * From February to March of {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 5 ({{ill|1785|ja}}), Nichishō (Nichishō) replaced the roof of the Tenman Tenjin-gū.
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)}} * {{ill|Ōno Tenmangū|ja|大野天満宮}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.honkouji.com/houmu/kigan/goukaku/index Honkōji Temple Homepage (Success Prayer)]

{{Multiple issues | Citation needed = November 7, 2015 | Wikify = November 7, 2015 }} {{Shrine | name = Tenman Tenjin-gū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tenman Tenjin-gū (Ichikawa) 06.jpg|240px]] | location = {{ill|Ichikawa City|ja|市川市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}, Ōno-machi 3-1948 | Map = | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} | Rank = | consecration_year = {{ill|Tengyō|ja|天慶}} 1 ({{ill|938|ja}}) | Main hall = | Alias = | Fuda-sho = | Annual festival = | Rituals = }}
'''Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (天満天神宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located on the site of the former {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)}} in {{ill|Ichikawa City|ja|市川市}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. The shrine is managed by {{ill|Honkōji Temple (Ichikawa)|ja|本光寺 (市川市)}}, a {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|日蓮宗}} temple. It is also known as '''Ōno Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (大野天満天神宮).
== Origins and History ==
According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Tengyō|ja|天慶}} 1 ({{ill|938|ja}}) by {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|平将門}}, who enshrined the deity of the {{ill|Kitano Tenmangū|ja|北野天満宮}} in Kyoto at this location. A hanging scroll depicting {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}, which includes a record of this origin, remains preserved. It is also noted that {{ill|Nichiren|ja|日蓮}} performed a dedication ceremony for the shrine. Later, Honkōji Temple became the shrine’s {{ill|bettō|ja|別当}} (caretaker temple), and it has remained under the temple’s management since the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代}}.
== Honkōji Temple Historical Records ==
* In {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永}} 7 ({{ill|1778|ja}}), the 19th head priest of Honkōji, Kangu-in {{ill|Nichishō (Honkōji)|ja|日正}} (Nichishō), restored the deity {{ill|Tenman Daijizai Tenjin|ja|天満大自在天神}}. * From February to March of {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明}} 5 ({{ill|1785|ja}}), Nichishō (Nichishō) replaced the roof of the Tenman Tenjin-gū.
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)}} * {{ill|Ōno Tenmangū|ja|大野天満宮}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.honkouji.com/houmu/kigan/goukaku/index Honkōji Temple Homepage (Success Prayer)]

{{Multiple issues | Citation needed = November 7, 2015 | Wikify = November 7, 2015 }} {{Shrine | name = Tenman Tenjin-gū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tenman Tenjin-gū (Ichikawa) 06.jpg|240px]] | location = {{ill|Ichikawa City|ja|市川市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}}, Ōno-machi 3-1948 | Map = | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} | Rank = | consecration_year = {{ill|Tengyō|ja|天慶|12=draft}} 1 ({{ill|938|ja}})|Main hall = | Alias = | Fuda-sho = | Annual festival = | Rituals =|12=draft}}
'''Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (天満天神宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located on the site of the former {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)|12=draft}} in {{ill|Ichikawa City|ja|市川市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県|12=draft}}. The shrine is managed by {{ill|Honkōji Temple (Ichikawa)|ja|本光寺 (市川市)|12=draft}}, a {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗|12=draft}} temple. It is also known as '''Ōno Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (大野天満天神宮).
== Origins and History ==
According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Tengyō|ja|天慶|12=draft}} 1 ({{ill|938|ja}}) by {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=draft}}, who enshrined the deity of the {{ill|Kitano Tenmangū|ja|北野天満宮|12=draft}} in Kyoto at this location. A hanging scroll depicting {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}}, which includes a record of this origin, remains preserved. It is also noted that {{ill|Nichiren|ja|Nichiren|12=draft}} performed a dedication ceremony for the shrine. Later, Honkōji Temple became the shrine’s {{ill|bettō|ja|別当|12=draft}} (caretaker temple), and it has remained under the temple’s management since the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治時代|12=draft}}.
== Honkōji Temple Historical Records ==
* In {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|12=draft}} 7 ({{ill|1778|ja}}), the 19th head priest of Honkōji, Kangu-in {{ill|Nichishō (Honkōji)|ja|日正|12=draft}} (Nichishō), restored the deity {{ill|Tenman Daijizai Tenjin|ja|天満大自在天神|12=draft}}. * From February to March of {{ill|Tenmei|ja|天明|12=draft}} 5 ({{ill|1785|ja}}), Nichishō (Nichishō) replaced the roof of the Tenman Tenjin-gū.
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ōno Tenmangū|ja|大野天満宮|12=draft}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.honkouji.com/houmu/kigan/goukaku/index Honkōji Temple Homepage (Success Prayer)]

revision.text:
{{複数の問題 |出典の明記=2015年11月7日 (土) 16:06 (UTC) |Wikify=2015年11月7日 (土) 16:06 (UTC) }} {{神社 |名称 = 天満天神宮 |画像 = [[画像:Tenman Tenjin-gū (Ichikawa) 06.jpg|240px]] |所在地 = [[千葉県]][[市川市]]大野町3-1948 |地図 = |祭神 = [[菅原道真]] |社格 = |創建 = [[天慶]]元年（[[938年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = }} '''天満天神宮'''（てんまんてんじんぐう）は、[[千葉県]][[市川市]]の[[大野城 (下総国)|大野城]]跡に鎮座する[[神社]]。[[日蓮宗]]の[[寺院]]の[[本光寺 (市川市)|本光寺]]が管理している。'''大野天満天神宮'''（おおのてんまんてんじんぐう）とも呼ばれる。
== 起源と歴史 == [[天慶]]元年（[[938年]]）、[[平将門]]が京都の[[北野天満宮]]をこの地に勧請したものであると伝えられる。[[菅原道真]]公の像を描いた[[掛軸]]に、その由来を記したものが残されている。また、[[日蓮]]聖人御開眼とも記されている。その後、本光寺が[[別当]]となり、[[明治]]時代以降もそのまま本光寺の管理下となった。
== 本光寺古文書 == * [[安永 (元号)|安永]]7年（[[1778年]]）、本光寺の第19世寛具院[[日正 (本光寺)|日正]]（[[日荘 (本光寺)|日荘]]）により、[[天満大自在天神|天神]]を再興。 * [[天明]]5年（[[1785年]]）2月より3月まで、本光寺の第19世寛具院日正（日荘）により、天満天神宮の屋根替。
== 関連項目 == * [[大野城 (下総国)]] * [[大野天満宮]]
== 外部リンク == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.honkouji.com/houmu/kigan/goukaku/index 本光寺ホームページ（合格祈願）] {{coord|35|45|17.5|N|139|57|25.7|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=千葉県}}
{{デフォルトソート:てんまんてんしんくう}} [[Category:千葉県の神社]] [[Category:天満宮]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:市川市の建築物]] [[Category:市川市の歴史]]

'''Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (天満天神宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on the site of the former {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|qq=|1=Ōno Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439549}} in [[:en:Ichikawa, Chiba|Ichikawa City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. The shrine is managed by {{ill|Honkōji Temple (Ichikawa)|ja|本光寺 (市川市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honkōji Temple (Ichikawa)|qq=|1=Honkō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519862}}, a {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗|1=Nichiren Shū|2=ja|3=日蓮宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren Buddhism|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q206611}} temple. It is also known as '''Ōno Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (大野天満天神宮).

== Origins and History ==

According to tradition, the shrine was established in [[:en:Tengyō|Tengyō]] 1 ([[:en:938|938]]) by {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who enshrined the deity of the [[:en:Kitano Tenmangū|Kitano Tenmangū]] in Kyoto at this location. A hanging scroll depicting {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, which includes a record of this origin, remains preserved. It is also noted that {{ill|Nichiren|ja|Nichiren|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} performed a dedication ceremony for the shrine. Later, Honkōji Temple became the shrine’s [[:en:Bettō|bettō]] (caretaker temple), and it has remained under the temple’s management since the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]].

== Honkōji Temple Historical Records ==

* In {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安永 (元号)|1=An'ei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193910}} 7 ([[:en:1778|1778]]), the 19th head priest of Honkōji, Kangu-in {{ill|Nichishō (Honkōji)|ja|日正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishō (Honkōji)|qq=|1=Nichishō (Honkōji)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997152}} (Nichishō), restored the deity [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenman Daijizai Tenjin]].
* From February to March of [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 5 ([[:en:1785|1785]]), Nichishō (Nichishō) replaced the roof of the Tenman Tenjin-gū.

== Related Articles ==

* {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|qq=|1=Ōno Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439549}}
* {{ill|Ōno Tenmangū|ja|大野天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Tenmangū|qq=|1=Ōno Tenmangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652634}}

== External Links ==

'''Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (天満天神宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on the site of the former {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|1=Ōno Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439549}} in [[:en:Ichikawa, Chiba|Ichikawa City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. The shrine is managed by {{ill|Honkōji Temple (Ichikawa)|ja|本光寺 (市川市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honkōji Temple (Ichikawa)|1=Honkō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519862}}, a {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗|1=Nichiren Shū|2=ja|3=日蓮宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren Buddhism|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q206611}} temple. It is also known as '''Ōno Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (大野天満天神宮).

== Origins and History ==

According to tradition, the shrine was established in [[:en:Tengyō|Tengyō]] 1 ([[:en:938|938]]) by {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who enshrined the deity of the [[:en:Kitano Tenmangū|Kitano Tenmangū]] in Kyoto at this location. It is also noted that {{ill|Nichiren|ja|Nichiren|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} performed a dedication ceremony for the shrine. Later, Honkōji Temple became the shrine’s [[:en:Bettō|bettō]] (caretaker temple), and it has remained under the temple’s management since the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]].

'''Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (天満天神宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on the site of the former {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|1=Ōno Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439549}} in [[:en:Ichikawa, Chiba|Ichikawa City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. The shrine is managed by {{ill|Honkōji Temple (Ichikawa)|ja|本光寺 (市川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honkōji Temple (Ichikawa)|1=Honkō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519862}}, a {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗|1=Nichiren Shū|2=ja|3=日蓮宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren Buddhism|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q206611}} temple. It is also known as '''Ōno Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (大野天満天神宮).

== Origins and History ==

According to tradition, the shrine was established in [[:en:Tengyō|Tengyō]] 1 ([[:en:938|938]]) by {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work}}, who enshrined the deity of the [[:en:Kitano Tenmangū|Kitano Tenmangū]] in Kyoto at this location. A hanging scroll depicting {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, which includes a record of this origin, remains preserved. It is also noted that {{ill|Nichiren|ja|Nichiren|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work}} performed a dedication ceremony for the shrine. Later, Honkōji Temple became the shrine’s [[:en:Bettō|bettō]] (caretaker temple), and it has remained under the temple’s management since the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]].

== Honkōji Temple Historical Records ==

* In {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安永 (元号)|1=An'ei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193910}} 7 ([[:en:1778|1778]]), the 19th head priest of Honkōji, Kangu-in {{ill|Nichishō (Honkōji)|ja|日正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishō (Honkōji)|1=Nichishō (Honkōji)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997152}} (Nichishō), restored the deity [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenman Daijizai Tenjin]].
* From February to March of [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 5 ([[:en:1785|1785]]), Nichishō (Nichishō) replaced the roof of the Tenman Tenjin-gū.

== Related Articles ==

* {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|1=Ōno Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439549}}

'''Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (天満天神宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on the site of the former {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|1=Ōno Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439549}} in [[:en:Ichikawa, Chiba|Ichikawa City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. The shrine is managed by {{ill|Honkōji Temple (Ichikawa)|ja|本光寺 (市川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honkōji Temple (Ichikawa)|1=Honkō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519862}}, a {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗|1=Nichiren Shū|2=ja|3=日蓮宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren Buddhism|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q206611}} temple. It is also known as '''Ōno Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (大野天満天神宮).

== Origins and History ==

According to tradition, the shrine was established in [[:en:Tengyō|Tengyō]] 1 ([[:en:938|938]]) by {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, who enshrined the deity of the [[:en:Kitano Tenmangū|Kitano Tenmangū]] in Kyoto at this location. A hanging scroll depicting {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, which includes a record of this origin, remains preserved. It is also noted that {{ill|Nichiren|ja|Nichiren|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} performed a dedication ceremony for the shrine. Later, Honkōji Temple became the shrine’s [[:en:Bettō|bettō]] (caretaker temple), and it has remained under the temple’s management since the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]].

== Honkōji Temple Historical Records ==

* In {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安永 (元号)|1=An'ei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193910}} 7 ([[:en:1778|1778]]), the 19th head priest of Honkōji, Kangu-in {{ill|Nichishō (Honkōji)|ja|日正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishō (Honkōji)|1=Nichishō (Honkōji)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997152}} (Nichishō), restored the deity [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenman Daijizai Tenjin]].
* From February to March of [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 5 ([[:en:1785|1785]]), Nichishō (Nichishō) replaced the roof of the Tenman Tenjin-gū.

== Related Articles ==

* {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|1=Ōno Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439549}}
* {{ill|Ōno Tenmangū|ja|大野天満宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Tenmangū|1=Ōno Tenmangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652634}}

== External Links ==

* In {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安永 (元号)|1=An'ei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193910}} 7 ([[:en:1778|1778]]), the 19th head priest of Honkōji, Kangu-in {{ill|Nichishō (Honkōji)|ja|日正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishō (Honkōji)|1=Nichishō (Honkōji)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997152}} (Nichishō), restored the deity [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenman Daijizai Tenjin]].
* From February to March of [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 5 ([[:en:1785|1785]]), Nichishō (Nichishō) replaced the roof of the Tenman Tenjin-gū.

'''Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (天満天神宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on the site of the former {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|1=Ōno Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439549}} in [[:en:Ichikawa, Chiba|Ichikawa City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. The shrine is managed by {{ill|Honkōji Temple (Ichikawa)|ja|本光寺 (市川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honkōji Temple (Ichikawa)|1=Honkō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519862}}, a {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗|1=Nichiren Shū|2=ja|3=日蓮宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren Buddhism|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q206611}} temple. It is also known as '''Ōno Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (大野天満天神宮).

== Origins and History ==

According to tradition, the shrine was established in [[:en:Tengyō|Tengyō]] 1 ([[:en:938|938]]) by {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, who enshrined the deity of the [[:en:Kitano Tenmangū|Kitano Tenmangū]] in Kyoto at this location. A hanging scroll depicting {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, which includes a record of this origin, remains preserved. It is also noted that {{ill|Nichiren|ja|Nichiren|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} performed a dedication ceremony for the shrine. Later, Honkōji Temple became the shrine’s [[:en:Bettō|bettō]] (caretaker temple), and it has remained under the temple’s management since the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]].

== Honkōji Temple Historical Records ==

* In {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安永 (元号)|1=An'ei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193910}} 7 ([[:en:1778|1778]]), the 19th head priest of Honkōji, Kangu-in {{ill|Nichishō (Honkōji)|ja|日正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishō (Honkōji)|1=Nichishō (Honkōji)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997152}} (Nichishō), restored the deity [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenman Daijizai Tenjin]].
* From February to March of [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 5 ([[:en:1785|1785]]), Nichishō (Nichishō) replaced the roof of the Tenman Tenjin-gū.

== Related Articles ==

'''Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (天満天神宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on the site of the former {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|1=Ōno Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439549}} in [[:en:Ichikawa, Chiba|Ichikawa City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. The shrine is managed by {{ill|Honkōji Temple (Ichikawa)|ja|本光寺 (市川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honkōji Temple (Ichikawa)|1=Honkō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519862}}, a {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗|1=Nichiren Shū|2=ja|3=日蓮宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren Buddhism|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q206611}} temple. It is also known as '''Ōno Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (大野天満天神宮).
== Origins and History ==
According to tradition, the shrine was established in [[:en:Tengyō|Tengyō]] 1 ([[:en:938|938]]) by {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, who enshrined the deity of the [[:en:Kitano Tenmangū|Kitano Tenmangū]] in Kyoto at this location. A hanging scroll depicting {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, which includes a record of this origin, remains preserved. It is also noted that {{ill|Nichiren|ja|Nichiren|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} performed a dedication ceremony for the shrine. Later, Honkōji Temple became the shrine’s [[:en:Bettō|bettō]] (caretaker temple), and it has remained under the temple’s management since the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]].
== Honkōji Temple Historical Records ==
* In {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安永 (元号)|1=An'ei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193910}} 7 ([[:en:1778|1778]]), the 19th head priest of Honkōji, Kangu-in {{ill|Nichishō (Honkōji)|ja|日正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishō (Honkōji)|1=Nichishō (Honkōji)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997152}} (Nichishō), restored the deity [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenman Daijizai Tenjin]]. * From February to March of [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 5 ([[:en:1785|1785]]), Nichishō (Nichishō) replaced the roof of the Tenman Tenjin-gū.
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|1=Ōno Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439549}} * {{ill|Ōno Tenmangū|ja|大野天満宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Tenmangū|1=Ōno Tenmangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652634}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.honkouji.com/houmu/kigan/goukaku/index Honkōji Temple Homepage (Success Prayer)]

* In {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安永 (元号)|1=An'ei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193910}} 7 ([[:en:1778|1778]]), the 19th head priest of Honkōji, Kangu-in {{ill|Nichishō (Honkōji)|ja|日正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishō (Honkōji)|1=Nichishō (Honkōji)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997152}} (Nichishō), restored the deity [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenman Daijizai Tenjin]].
* From February to March of [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 5 ([[:en:1785|1785]]), Nichishō (Nichishō) replaced the roof of the Tenman Tenjin-gū.

'''Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (天満天神宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on the site of the former {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|1=Ōno Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439549}} in [[:en:Ichikawa, Chiba|Ichikawa City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. The shrine is managed by {{ill|Honkōji Temple (Ichikawa)|ja|本光寺 (市川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honkōji Temple (Ichikawa)|1=Honkō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519862}}, a {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗|1=Nichiren Shū|2=ja|3=日蓮宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren Buddhism|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q206611}} temple. It is also known as '''Ōno Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (大野天満天神宮).

== Origins and History ==

According to tradition, the shrine was established in [[:en:Tengyō|Tengyō]] 1 ([[:en:938|938]]) by {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, who enshrined the deity of the [[:en:Kitano Tenmangū|Kitano Tenmangū]] in Kyoto at this location. A hanging scroll depicting {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, which includes a record of this origin, remains preserved. It is also noted that {{ill|Nichiren|ja|Nichiren|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} performed a dedication ceremony for the shrine. Later, Honkōji Temple became the shrine’s [[:en:Bettō|bettō]] (caretaker temple), and it has remained under the temple’s management since the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]].

== Honkōji Temple Historical Records ==

* In {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安永 (元号)|1=An'ei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193910}} 7 ([[:en:1778|1778]]), the 19th head priest of Honkōji, Kangu-in {{ill|Nichishō (Honkōji)|ja|日正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishō (Honkōji)|1=Nichishō (Honkōji)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997152}} (Nichishō), restored the deity [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenman Daijizai Tenjin]].
* From February to March of [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 5 ([[:en:1785|1785]]), Nichishō (Nichishō) replaced the roof of the Tenman Tenjin-gū.

== Related Articles ==

* {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|1=Ōno Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439549}}

'''Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (天満天神宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on the site of the former {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|1=Ōno Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439549}} in [[:en:Ichikawa, Chiba|Ichikawa City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. The shrine is managed by {{ill|Honkōji Temple (Ichikawa)|ja|本光寺 (市川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honkōji Temple (Ichikawa)|1=Honkō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519862}}, a {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗|1=Nichiren Shū|2=ja|3=日蓮宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren Buddhism|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q206611}} temple. It is also known as '''Ōno Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (大野天満天神宮).
== Origins and History ==
According to tradition, the shrine was established in [[:en:Tengyō|Tengyō]] 1 ([[:en:938|938]]) by {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, who enshrined the deity of the [[:en:Kitano Tenmangū|Kitano Tenmangū]] in Kyoto at this location. A hanging scroll depicting {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, which includes a record of this origin, remains preserved. It is also noted that {{ill|Nichiren|ja|Nichiren|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} performed a dedication ceremony for the shrine. Later, Honkōji Temple became the shrine’s [[:en:Bettō|bettō]] (caretaker temple), and it has remained under the temple’s management since the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]].
== Honkōji Temple Historical Records ==
* In {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安永 (元号)|1=An'ei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193910}} 7 ([[:en:1778|1778]]), the 19th head priest of Honkōji, Kangu-in {{ill|Nichishō (Honkōji)|ja|日正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishō (Honkōji)|1=Nichishō (Honkōji)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997152}} (Nichishō), restored the deity [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenman Daijizai Tenjin]]. * From February to March of [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 5 ([[:en:1785|1785]]), Nichishō (Nichishō) replaced the roof of the Tenman Tenjin-gū.
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|1=Ōno Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439549}} * {{ill|Ōno Tenmangū|ja|大野天満宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Tenmangū|1=Ōno Tenmangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652634}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.honkouji.com/houmu/kigan/goukaku/index Honkōji Temple Homepage (Success Prayer)]

== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|1=Ōno Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439549}} * {{ill|Ōno Tenmangū|ja|大野天満宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Tenmangū|1=Ōno Tenmangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652634}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tenman Tenjin-gū (Ichikawa)]][[ja:天満天神宮 (市川市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tenman Tenjin-gū (Ichikawa)]][[ja:天満天神宮 (市川市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tenman Tenjin-gū (Ichikawa)]][[ja:天満天神宮 (市川市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q65259223]] Wikidata name: Tenman Tenjin-gū Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:30, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Multiple issues | Citation needed = November 7, 2015 | Wikify = November 7, 2015 }} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenman Tenjin-gū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tenman Tenjin-gū (Ichikawa) 06.jpg|240px]] | location = [[:en:Ichikawa, Chiba|Ichikawa City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Ōno-machi 3-1948 | Map = | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | Rank = | consecration_year = [[:en:Tengyō|Tengyō]] 1 ([[:en:938|938]])|Main hall = | Alias = | Fuda-sho = | Annual festival = | Rituals =|qq=draft}}
'''Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (天満天神宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located on the site of the former {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|1=Ōno Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439549}} in [[:en:Ichikawa, Chiba|Ichikawa City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. The shrine is managed by {{ill|Honkōji Temple (Ichikawa)|ja|本光寺 (市川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honkōji Temple (Ichikawa)|1=Honkō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519862}}, a {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗|1=Nichiren Shū|2=ja|3=日蓮宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren Buddhism|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q206611}} temple. It is also known as '''Ōno Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (大野天満天神宮).
== Origins and History ==
According to tradition, the shrine was established in [[:en:Tengyō|Tengyō]] 1 ([[:en:938|938]]) by {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, who enshrined the deity of the [[:en:Kitano Tenmangū|Kitano Tenmangū]] in Kyoto at this location. A hanging scroll depicting {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, which includes a record of this origin, remains preserved. It is also noted that {{ill|Nichiren|ja|Nichiren|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} performed a dedication ceremony for the shrine. Later, Honkōji Temple became the shrine’s [[:en:Bettō|bettō]] (caretaker temple), and it has remained under the temple’s management since the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]].
== Honkōji Temple Historical Records ==
* In {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安永 (元号)|1=An'ei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193910}} 7 ([[:en:1778|1778]]), the 19th head priest of Honkōji, Kangu-in {{ill|Nichishō (Honkōji)|ja|日正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishō (Honkōji)|1=Nichishō (Honkōji)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997152}} (Nichishō), restored the deity [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenman Daijizai Tenjin]]. * From February to March of [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 5 ([[:en:1785|1785]]), Nichishō (Nichishō) replaced the roof of the Tenman Tenjin-gū.

It is also known as '''Ōno Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (大野天満天神宮).
== Origins and History ==
According to tradition, the shrine was established in [[:en:Tengyō|Tengyō]] 1 ([[:en:938|938]]) by {{ill|Taira no Masakado|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, who enshrined the deity of the [[:en:Kitano Tenmangū|Kitano Tenmangū]] in Kyoto at this location. A hanging scroll depicting {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, which includes a record of this origin, remains preserved. It is also noted that {{ill|Nichiren|ja|Nichiren|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} performed a dedication ceremony for the shrine. Later, Honkōji Temple became the shrine’s [[:en:Bettō|bettō]] (caretaker temple), and it has remained under the temple’s management since the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]].
== Honkōji Temple Historical Records ==
* In {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安永 (元号)|1=An'ei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193910}} 7 ([[:en:1778|1778]]), the 19th head priest of Honkōji, Kangu-in {{ill|Nichishō (Honkōji)|ja|日正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishō (Honkōji)|1=Nichishō (Honkōji)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997152}} (Nichishō), restored the deity [[:en:Tenjin (kami)|Tenman Daijizai Tenjin]]. * From February to March of [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 5 ([[:en:1785|1785]]), Nichishō (Nichishō) replaced the roof of the Tenman Tenjin-gū.
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|1=Ōno Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439549}} * {{ill|Ōno Tenmangū|ja|大野天満宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Tenmangū|1=Ōno Tenmangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652634}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.honkouji.com/houmu/kigan/goukaku/index Honkōji Temple Homepage (Success Prayer)]

* In {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安永 (元号)|1=An'ei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193910}} 7 ({{ill|1778|en|1778|WD=Q7713|lt=1778}}), the 19th head priest of Honkōji, Kangu-in {{ill|Nichishō (Honkōji)|ja|日正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishō (Honkōji)|1=Nichishō (Honkōji)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997152}} (Nichishō), restored the deity {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|WD=Q1753428|lt=Tenman Daijizai Tenjin}}. * From February to March of {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei}} 5 ({{ill|1785|en|1785|WD=Q7756|lt=1785}}), Nichishō (Nichishō) replaced the roof of the Tenman Tenjin-gū.
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|1=Ōno Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439549}} * {{ill|Ōno Tenmangū|ja|大野天満宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Tenmangū|1=Ōno Tenmangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652634}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tenman Tenjin-gū (Ichikawa)]][[ja:天満天神宮 (市川市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tenman Tenjin-gū (Ichikawa)]][[ja:天満天神宮 (市川市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tenman Tenjin-gū (Ichikawa)]][[ja:天満天神宮 (市川市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q65259223]] Wikidata name: Tenman Tenjin-gū Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:30, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Multiple issues | Citation needed = November 7, 2015 | Wikify = November 7, 2015 }} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenman Tenjin-gū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tenman Tenjin-gū (Ichikawa) 06.jpg|240px]] | location = {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en|Ichikawa, Chiba|WD=Q209785|lt=Ichikawa City}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, Ōno-machi 3-1948 | Map = | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | Rank = | consecration_year = {{ill|Tengyō|en|Tengyō|WD=Q1327340|lt=Tengyō}} 1 ({{ill|938|en|938|WD=Q24794|lt=938}})|Main hall = | Alias = | Fuda-sho = | Annual festival = | Rituals =|qq=draft}}
'''Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (天満天神宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located on the site of the former {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|1=Ōno Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439549}} in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en|Ichikawa, Chiba|WD=Q209785|lt=Ichikawa City}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}. The shrine is managed by {{ill|Honkōji Temple (Ichikawa)|ja|本光寺 (市川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honkōji Temple (Ichikawa)|1=Honkō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519862}}, a {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗|1=Nichiren Shū|2=ja|3=日蓮宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren Buddhism|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q206611}} temple. It is also known as '''Ōno Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (大野天満天神宮).
== Origins and History ==
According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Tengyō|en|Tengyō|WD=Q1327340|lt=Tengyō}} 1 ({{ill|938|en|938|WD=Q24794|lt=938}}) by {{ill|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, who enshrined the deity of the {{ill|Kitano Tenmangū|en|Kitano Tenmangū|WD=Q662176|lt=Kitano Tenmangū}} in Kyoto at this location. A hanging scroll depicting {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, which includes a record of this origin, remains preserved. It is also noted that {{ill|Nichiren|1=Nichiren|2=ja|3=日蓮|4=ko|5=니치렌|6=de|7=Nichiren|8=es|9=Nichiren|10=fr|11=Nichiren|12=it|13=Nichiren|14=nl|15=Nichiren|16=no|17=Nichiren|lt=Nichiren|WD=Q311478ja|Nichiren|1=Nichiren|2=ja|3=日蓮|4=ko|5=니치렌|6=de|7=Nichiren|8=es|9=Nichiren|10=fr|11=Nichiren|12=it|13=Nichiren|14=nl|15=Nichiren|16=no|17=Nichiren|lt=Nichiren|WD=Q31147812=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} performed a dedication ceremony for the shrine. Later, Honkōji Temple became the shrine’s {{ill|Bettō|en|Bettō|WD=Q2900274|lt=bettō}} (caretaker temple), and it has remained under the temple’s management since the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}.
== Honkōji Temple Historical Records ==
* In {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安永 (元号)|1=An'ei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193910}} 7 ({{ill|1778|en|1778|WD=Q7713|lt=1778}}), the 19th head priest of Honkōji, Kangu-in {{ill|Nichishō (Honkōji)|ja|日正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishō (Honkōji)|1=Nichishō (Honkōji)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997152}} (Nichishō), restored the deity {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|WD=Q1753428|lt=Tenman Daijizai Tenjin}}. * From February to March of {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei}} 5 ({{ill|1785|en|1785|WD=Q7756|lt=1785}}), Nichishō (Nichishō) replaced the roof of the Tenman Tenjin-gū.
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|1=Ōno Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439549}} * {{ill|Ōno Tenmangū|ja|大野天満宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Tenmangū|1=Ōno Tenmangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652634}}

It is also noted that {{ill|Nichiren|1=Nichiren|2=ja|3=日蓮|4=ko|5=니치렌|6=de|7=Nichiren|8=es|9=Nichiren|10=fr|11=Nichiren|12=it|13=Nichiren|14=nl|15=Nichiren|16=no|17=Nichiren|lt=Nichiren|WD=Q311478ja|Nichiren|1=Nichiren|2=ja|3=日蓮|4=ko|5=니치렌|6=de|7=Nichiren|8=es|9=Nichiren|10=fr|11=Nichiren|12=it|13=Nichiren|14=nl|15=Nichiren|16=no|17=Nichiren|lt=Nichiren|WD=Q31147812=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} performed a dedication ceremony for the shrine. Later, Honkōji Temple became the shrine’s {{ill|Bettō|en|Bettō|WD=Q2900274|lt=bettō}} (caretaker temple), and it has remained under the temple’s management since the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}.
== Honkōji Temple Historical Records ==
* In {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安永 (元号)|1=An'ei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193910}} 7 ({{ill|1778|en|1778|WD=Q7713|lt=1778}}), the 19th head priest of Honkōji, Kangu-in {{ill|Nichishō (Honkōji)|ja|日正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishō (Honkōji)|1=Nichishō (Honkōji)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997152}} (Nichishō), restored the deity {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|WD=Q1753428|lt=Tenman Daijizai Tenjin}}. * From February to March of {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei}} 5 ({{ill|1785|en|1785|WD=Q7756|lt=1785}}), Nichishō (Nichishō) replaced the roof of the Tenman Tenjin-gū.

{{Multiple issues | Citation needed = November 7, 2015 | Wikify = November 7, 2015 }} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tenman Tenjin-gū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tenman Tenjin-gū (Ichikawa) 06.jpg|240px]] | location = {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en|Ichikawa, Chiba|WD=Q209785|lt=Ichikawa City}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, Ōno-machi 3-1948 | Map = | deity = {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} | Rank = | consecration_year = {{ill|Tengyō|en|Tengyō|WD=Q1327340|lt=Tengyō}} 1 ({{ill|938|en|938|WD=Q24794|lt=938}})|Main hall = | Alias = | Fuda-sho = | Annual festival = | Rituals =|qq=draft}}
'''Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (天満天神宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located on the site of the former {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|1=Ōno Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439549}} in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en|Ichikawa, Chiba|WD=Q209785|lt=Ichikawa City}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}. The shrine is managed by {{ill|Honkōji Temple (Ichikawa)|ja|本光寺 (市川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honkōji Temple (Ichikawa)|1=Honkō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519862}}, a {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗|1=Nichiren Shū|2=ja|3=日蓮宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren Buddhism|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q206611}} temple. It is also known as '''Ōno Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (大野天満天神宮).
== Origins and History ==
According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Tengyō|en|Tengyō|WD=Q1327340|lt=Tengyō}} 1 ({{ill|938|en|938|WD=Q24794|lt=938}}) by {{ill|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, who enshrined the deity of the {{ill|Kitano Tenmangū|en|Kitano Tenmangū|WD=Q662176|lt=Kitano Tenmangū}} in Kyoto at this location. A hanging scroll depicting {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, which includes a record of this origin, remains preserved. It is also noted that {{ill|Nichiren|1=Nichiren|2=ja|3=日蓮|4=ko|5=니치렌|6=de|7=Nichiren|8=es|9=Nichiren|10=fr|11=Nichiren|12=it|13=Nichiren|14=nl|15=Nichiren|16=no|17=Nichiren|lt=Nichiren|WD=Q311478ja|Nichiren|1=Nichiren|2=ja|3=日蓮|4=ko|5=니치렌|6=de|7=Nichiren|8=es|9=Nichiren|10=fr|11=Nichiren|12=it|13=Nichiren|14=nl|15=Nichiren|16=no|17=Nichiren|lt=Nichiren|WD=Q31147812=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} performed a dedication ceremony for the shrine. Later, Honkōji Temple became the shrine’s {{ill|Bettō|en|Bettō|WD=Q2900274|lt=bettō}} (caretaker temple), and it has remained under the temple’s management since the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}.
== Honkōji Temple Historical Records ==
* In {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安永 (元号)|1=An'ei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193910}} 7 ({{ill|1778|en|1778|WD=Q7713|lt=1778}}), the 19th head priest of Honkōji, Kangu-in {{ill|Nichishō (Honkōji)|ja|日正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishō (Honkōji)|1=Nichishō (Honkōji)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997152}} (Nichishō), restored the deity {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|WD=Q1753428|lt=Tenman Daijizai Tenjin}}. * From February to March of {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei}} 5 ({{ill|1785|en|1785|WD=Q7756|lt=1785}}), Nichishō (Nichishō) replaced the roof of the Tenman Tenjin-gū.

According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Tengyō|en|Tengyō|WD=Q1327340|lt=Tengyō|1=Tengyō}} 1 ({{ill|938|en|938|WD=Q24794|lt=938|1=938}}) by {{ill|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, who enshrined the deity of the {{ill|Kitano Tenmangū|en|Kitano Tenmangū|WD=Q662176|lt=Kitano Tenmangū|1=Kitano Tenmangū}} in Kyoto at this location. A hanging scroll depicting {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, which includes a record of this origin, remains preserved. It is also noted that {{ill|Nichiren|1=Nichiren|2=ja|3=日蓮|4=ko|5=니치렌|6=de|7=Nichiren|8=es|9=Nichiren|10=fr|11=Nichiren|12=it|13=Nichiren|14=nl|15=Nichiren|16=no|17=Nichiren|lt=Nichiren|WD=Q311478ja|Nichiren|1=Nichiren|2=ja|3=日蓮|4=ko|5=니치렌|6=de|7=Nichiren|8=es|9=Nichiren|10=fr|11=Nichiren|12=it|13=Nichiren|14=nl|15=Nichiren|16=no|17=Nichiren|lt=Nichiren|WD=Q31147812=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} performed a dedication ceremony for the shrine. Later, Honkōji Temple became the shrine’s {{ill|Bettō|en|Bettō|WD=Q2900274|lt=bettō|1=Bettō}} (caretaker temple), and it has remained under the temple’s management since the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}.
== Honkōji Temple Historical Records ==
* In {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安永 (元号)|1=An'ei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193910}} 7 ({{ill|1778|en|1778|WD=Q7713|lt=1778|1=1778}}), the 19th head priest of Honkōji, Kangu-in {{ill|Nichishō (Honkōji)|ja|日正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishō (Honkōji)|1=Q23997152|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997152}} (Nichishō), restored the deity {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|WD=Q1753428|lt=Tenman Daijizai Tenjin|1=Tenjin (kami)}}. * From February to March of {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} 5 ({{ill|1785|en|1785|WD=Q7756|lt=1785|1=1785}}), Nichishō (Nichishō) replaced the roof of the Tenman Tenjin-gū.

'''Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (天満天神宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located on the site of the former {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|1=Ōno Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439549}} in {{ill|Ichikawa, Chiba|en|Ichikawa, Chiba|WD=Q209785|lt=Ichikawa City|1=Ichikawa, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}. The shrine is managed by {{ill|Honkōji Temple (Ichikawa)|ja|本光寺 (市川市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honkōji Temple (Ichikawa)|1=Honkō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519862}}, a {{ill|Nichiren Buddhism|ja|Nichiren宗|1=Nichiren-shū|2=ja|3=日蓮宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren Buddhism|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q206611}} temple. It is also known as '''Ōno Tenman Tenjin-gū''' (大野天満天神宮).
== Origins and History ==
According to tradition, the shrine was established in {{ill|Tengyō|en|Tengyō|WD=Q1327340|lt=Tengyō|1=Tengyō}} 1 ({{ill|938|en|938|WD=Q24794|lt=938|1=938}}) by {{ill|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|WD=Q1207652|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, who enshrined the deity of the {{ill|Kitano Tenmangū|en|Kitano Tenmangū|WD=Q662176|lt=Kitano Tenmangū|1=Kitano Tenmangū}} in Kyoto at this location. A hanging scroll depicting {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}, which includes a record of this origin, remains preserved. It is also noted that {{ill|Nichiren|1=Nichiren|2=ja|3=日蓮|4=ko|5=니치렌|6=de|7=Nichiren|8=es|9=Nichiren|10=fr|11=Nichiren|12=it|13=Nichiren|14=nl|15=Nichiren|16=no|17=Nichiren|lt=Nichiren|WD=Q311478ja|Nichiren|1=Nichiren|2=ja|3=日蓮|4=ko|5=니치렌|6=de|7=Nichiren|8=es|9=Nichiren|10=fr|11=Nichiren|12=it|13=Nichiren|14=nl|15=Nichiren|16=no|17=Nichiren|lt=Nichiren|WD=Q31147812=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichiren|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} performed a dedication ceremony for the shrine. Later, Honkōji Temple became the shrine’s {{ill|Bettō|en|Bettō|WD=Q2900274|lt=bettō|1=Bettō}} (caretaker temple), and it has remained under the temple’s management since the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}.
== Honkōji Temple Historical Records ==
* In {{ill|An'ei|ja|安永|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/An'ei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安永 (元号)|1=An'ei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1193910}} 7 ({{ill|1778|en|1778|WD=Q7713|lt=1778|1=1778}}), the 19th head priest of Honkōji, Kangu-in {{ill|Nichishō (Honkōji)|ja|日正|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichishō (Honkōji)|1=Q23997152|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997152}} (Nichishō), restored the deity {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|WD=Q1753428|lt=Tenman Daijizai Tenjin|1=Tenjin (kami)}}. * From February to March of {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} 5 ({{ill|1785|en|1785|WD=Q7756|lt=1785|1=1785}}), Nichishō (Nichishō) replaced the roof of the Tenman Tenjin-gū.
== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|ja|大野城 (下総国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Castle (Shimōsa Province)|1=Ōno Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439549}} * {{ill|Ōno Tenmangū|ja|大野天満宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōno Tenmangū|1=Ōno Tenmangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21652634}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat}} * [http://www.honkouji.com/houmu/kigan/goukaku/index Honkōji Temple Homepage (Success Prayer)]
